# Figure description: Honeyview_regional geology plan for combined reporting.jpg

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
Geological map overlaid with mineral exploration tenure boundaries (exploration licence outlines). This is a combined geology and tenement map showing bedrock geology draped with cadastral exploration licence polygons.

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers a portion of the **Murchison region of Western Australia**, centered on what appears to be the **Gascoyne/Murchison** geological province. The map sheet referenced appears to be **C50/A2, C57/D38, A2096** (visible in the bottom-right legend/title block). Two exploration licence tenements are prominently outlined:
- **E 2000948** (larger, northern polygon)
- **E 2000996** (smaller, central-southern polygon)

The map covers approximately **6,854,000 mN to 6,996,000 mN** northing range (MGA grid), suggesting a north-south extent of roughly 140 km.

## Key Content
**Geology:**
- The map displays multicoloured bedrock geology units typical of a Proterozoic/Archaean terrane in the Murchison
- **Green/olive-coloured units** dominate the central corridor, likely representing **greenstone belt lithologies** (mafic-ultramafic volcanics, BIF, or metasediments)
- **Yellow/pale units** to the east likely represent **granitic or felsic intrusive rocks**
- **Pink/magenta units** in the upper-left corner likely represent **granites or felsic volcanics**
- **Darker brown/orange patches** suggest laterite, ironstone, or oxide zones
- A pronounced **NNW-trending structural corridor** of greenstone lithologies runs through the central portion of the map — this is characteristic of Murchison greenstone belts (possibly the **Meekatharra-Wydgee Belt** or similar)

**Structural Features:**
- Multiple **curvilinear contour lines** (likely aeromagnetic or gravity isolines) cross the map in a broadly NW-SE orientation, suggesting geophysical data is underlaid or referenced
- The structural grain of geology clearly trends **NNW-SSE**, consistent with regional Yilgarn/Murchison deformation

**Tenement Boundaries:**
- **E 2000948** covers a large rectangular area in the north-central portion, overlapping both greenstone and granite terrain
- **E 2000996** is a smaller, irregular polygon situated over the greenstone corridor to the south, likely targeting the most prospective lithological units

Visible text includes:
- **Title block (bottom right):** "Greengate Gold / Castlewood Recycling Australia / Geology Map / C50/A2 C57/D38 A2096"
- **Tenement labels:** "E 2000948" (central-north), "E 2000096" (central-south)

## Research Value
This figure establishes the **spatial relationship between exploration licence boundaries and underlying bedrock geology**, allowing a geologist to rapidly assess which lithological units are being targeted. The positioning of **E 2000996** directly over the NNW-trending greenstone corridor strongly suggests the tenement is targeting **gold or base metal mineralisation** associated with greenstone-hosted systems (e.g., BIF-hosted gold, komatiite-hosted nickel, or VMS). Combined with the apparent geophysical contour overlay, this map helps a researcher understand both the **structural controls on mineralisation** and the **strategic rationale for the tenement applications** made by Castlewood Recycling Australia / Greengate Gold in this part of the Murchison.
